formula: SiO2 (approximately 70–75%) with Al2O3, FeO, Fe2O3, MgO, CaO, K2O, Na2O
_all_text: Obsidian obsidian SiO2 (approximately 70–75%) with Al2O3, FeO, Fe2O3, MgO, CaO, K2O, Na2O Color and optical effects depend on Fe content, gas bubble distribution, and crystallite inclusions. Black obsidian is colored by iron and magnetite. Snowflake obsidian contains spherulites of cristobalite (white). Rainbow obsidian gets its iridescence from nanolaminate magnetite layers. Mahogany obsidian has reddish-brown bands from Fe oxidation. Obsidian is pure volcanic glass — the most visually simple and the most dramatically effective black mineral material. Its jet-black vitreous surface reflects light cleanly. Rainbow varieties produce full spectral color from thin-film magnetite layers. Apache tears appear opaque but transmit brownish light when held up. Tezcatlipoca, the Aztec god of night, sorcery, and the north, is symbolized by a black obsidian smoking mirror — his attribute and the means by which he could see all things in the universe including human sin and fate. Obsidian mirrors were used by priests for scrying and divination. The Apache tears legend holds that women of an Apache band wept when their warriors were killed in battle; their tears fell and crystallized as obsidian nodules. In modern metaphysical tradition, obsidian is a 'truth stone' associated with shadow work. truth-revealing mirror the void and shadow-self protection from negative energy warrior sharpness volcanic creation and destruction Stone tools — blades, knives, scrapers (Lower Paleolithic to historically in some regions) Surgical blades (modern surgical use — some cardiothoracic surgeons prefer obsidian scalpels) Mirrors (Aztec; polished obsidian mirrors for divination) Trade commodity (obsidian exchange networks documented from Neolithic through Bronze Age) Ritual and ceremonial objects (Aztec, Mesoamerican, and many other cultures) Arrowheads and projectile points volcanic glass obsidian blade Apache tears snowflake obsidian rainbow obsidian Aztec mirror Tezcatlipoca stone tool Neolithic obsidian trade mineraloid volcanic-glass amorphous black silica tool-stone aztec ancient-trade surgical high-priority volcanic-void obsidian-mirror razor-blade-truth shadow-surface night-glass black dark-brown
